title: BAD TIMES
pros: Worked there 20 years. Great pay and benefits.
cons: Product of Immelt's lack of foresight. He put the Power Division in an almost impossible position to be profitable. I'm a product job elimination GE Power had seen in 25-30 years or more. 14 OAs were eliminated from Greenville campus on same say...December 18. My boss's boss wasn't told until 2 days prior and both were shocked. I was 59-1/2 so I was able to start my pension...most others were not so lucky. I always said they'd have to take me out feet first because I never intended to leave. I was treated fairly but was very sad to leave. LOVED MY TEAM (Repair Technolog IS THE BEST) AND MY JOB. Hope things are doing ok with new Vernova setup.

title: Great work experience
pros: Upon commencing employment, you find yourself within a company boasting a history exceeding 130 years. The organization's deeply ingrained structure and mature operational processes become immediately evident, offering a distinct experience of working within a long-established entity. The company actively promotes internal mobility, encouraging employees to explore diverse roles after approximately eighteen months. This policy enables you to cultivate a broad range of skills and acquire experience across various departments. Consequently, a five-to-ten-year tenure within this organization can provide an experience comparable to working in three or four separate companies in different capacities. This emphasis on internal rotation necessitates adaptability and a readiness to embrace new challenges and opportunities.
cons: The Human Resources department exerts excessive influence. Furthermore, salaries are not competitive when compared to those offered by other private companies producing similar products.

title: Great Learning and Work Environment
pros: I got the opportunity to learn a plethora of technical skills in 2 years working with GE that I still use at other employers to this day. The work environment was friendly and encouraging. Never I had a day where I dreaded going into work or encountering an individual. Everyone was held at a high responsibility for their own work and attitude which was a huge plus for those that actually came in to work.
cons: Simply put, the pay and review process. As an exceptional learner and worker I quickly learned the required building process for much of the equipment with a high level of performance. We had a (Level 1 - 4) scale this was tracked by which effected your pay. I felt that my scale never was updated or reflected my work and many of my peers felt the same way. They would constantly come up with excuses for why not to increase the pay while we churned out record profits every year and always paid the "shareholders" (the ones that don't work ) more and more.

title: Amazing WLB but pay can be tightly controlled
body: Amazing WLB but pay can be tightly controlled
pros: - Flexible deadlines - Remote working is supported - Company is large enough to have career tracks into Senior Staff or Principal titles - Well established company with well-defined organizations. Easy to start as new hire and begin work immediately. - Pay is not of the top Big Tech salaries, but 40 hour/work week is highly respected. You may have ample opportunity and time for a side business to generate additional income such as selling your hobbies.
cons: - Annual bonus is on low end (10%) - Annual bonus is dependent on company's profit performance - Must make strong case for making jump from Staff to Sr. Staff. Bigger hurdle than jumping from Sr. Staff to Principal. - Don't count on company to automatically give promotions. MUST make strong case and advocate yourself, or switch jobs.

body: A great job for those looking for stability but without a lot of opportunity for professional growth
pros: The company has excellent benefits and for the most part the team you work with will be very professional. Excellent work/life balance. I greatly enjoyed working with the team at GE.
cons: I found the division I worked for not particularly innovative. The systems (project data management, design etc) were slow and cumbersome making much of the time spent on work feel unproductive and tedious. If you are someone who appreciates efficiency and wants to do great work, you may find this frustrating.

And was terminated, was less then 5 days later when my assigned doctor wrote my plain and release and sent to HR. And was told to reapply. I didn't understand why I needed to reapply to a job I already had. Of course I reapplied but was told my application was not excepted and was not hired. The stress of 8 hours a day 5 days a week it's customers calling because there appliances don't work. You will learn that GE appliances are not made well anymore and there quality control is deficient.

title: Make sure you have it ALL IN WRITING in your offer letter before you sign is all I can say really.
body: Make sure you have it ALL IN WRITING in your offer letter before you sign is all I can say really.
pros: I really love my coworkers ! I can tell you that everyone really tries to do their best for this company. I look forward to working with my dynamic team everyday.
cons: I am still upset (after all these years-lol) that many things that were verbally promised in the phone interview; were never given in the offer letter. That includes the compensation. HR denies any wrong doing and of course how do you prove what happened on a phone call? So be leery and get it ALL IN WRITING before accepting ANY offer. I learned the hard way. Once you sign; they have you caught at that point. This also has been told to me by several coworkers (a whole group now) who had the same instance happen to them too in this particular department. (It's just not me being a complainer here.) That has been one of my main negative sticking points for me about their integrity; which is one of their main principles. It's a shame. I wish I could give a stellar review.

title: Many Lessons Learned
pros: Decent pay and benefits when I started.
cons: The move to the Grove was unnecessary and expensive. Employees had to pay for parking and the office was eventually closed anyway due to GE Digital's failure. All remaining offices in Miami are closing as of 2023 in favor of other sites such as Monterrey, Mexico. Where there were originally over 100 Miami technicians, there are only about 25. As the office was expanding, a lot of office politics set in. You didn't know who to trust. Managers played favorites and did not have technical expertise. A manager having an intimate relationship with a direct report. A transgendered employee reports you to HR if you unknowingly refer to them as the incorrect pronouns. I didn't realize how toxic this workplace was until I left. The actual work was stressing. Lots of micromanaging and you were expected to take 2 or 3 chats at a time. There's also no room to grow in the company and a lot of the applications are so proprietary, you'll have little value to other organizations.

title: Decent place until it isn't
pros: Benefits were very strong and reasonably priced, the pay was decent as well, could've been more but there are definitely worse in the area.
cons: If you're within the first 6th months you are completely at the whim of their decisions on attendance. I had a vacation day scheduled and was bumped off without ever having knowledge of it, and was penalized for it with no resolution. I also had to miss a few days due to a doctor telling me I couldn't work, and they shrugged their shoulders and told me they could make a note of it but it ultimately didn't matter. While I did miss some time due to reasons within my control, ultimately I was fired because of the points accrued due to a doctor telling me I couldn't go to work and due to the company's error that I had no control over. Not to mention the outlandish expectations and the complete ignorance of certain management towards what goes on on the production floor.

title: Unambitious company
pros: This might be a pro for some people (wasn't for me) the company was full of very unambitious people so it's a good place to work if you want to just punch a clock. Some of the people there were nice. I had a lot of latitude to do what I thought was important.
cons: The pay was very bad. Everyone there said you're lucky if you get more than a 2% raise every year. HR was very dishonest and deducted a future merit increase from a promotion I earned. My supervisor withheld emails that showed that HR screwed me out of a raise. Pushing for even small low effort improvements to processes was next to impossible.
